items.mistake: Concluding results without a clean top-to-bottom run.
items.fix: Restart & Run All before trusting or sharing.
items.why: Hidden state makes broken notebooks look correct.
items.example: Your model accuracy 'looks great' — but a cell defining `X_train` was edited and never re-run. Restart & Run All would crash on `NameError` or produce a different (real) number.

items.mistake: Copy-pasting the same function across many notebooks.
items.fix: Extract it into a .py module and import it.
items.why: Duplication drifts and can't be tested centrally.
items.example: `chunk_text(...)` lives in 5 notebooks; you fix a bug in one. Six months later, you can't remember which version is correct. Move it to `ragapp/chunker.py`, import it everywhere, write one unit test.

items.mistake: Committing notebooks with all outputs embedded.
items.fix: Strip outputs (nbstripout) and/or pair a .py with jupytext.
items.why: Outputs bloat diffs and may contain responses/keys.
items.example: A 50-cell notebook with rich outputs is a 12 MB JSON blob → unreadable diff. `nbstripout --install` clears outputs on every commit; diffs now show only what changed in cells.

items.mistake: Hard-coding credentials or pasting large data inline.
items.fix: Read keys from the environment; keep big data in external storage.
items.why: Saved notebooks leak secrets and become huge.
items.example:
```python
# BAD: leaks on every commit
key = 'sk-abc123...'

# GOOD: loaded at runtime, never in the file
key = os.environ['OPENAI_API_KEY']
```
